reasoning: A rate cut of 50+ bps at a single regular FOMC meeting is rare and typically reserved for economic emergencies. January meetings are standard FOMC meetings, and current market expectations for early 2026 are pointing towards a stabilizing or very gradually declining rate environment. While an emergency could happen at any time, the base probability of a single 50+ bps cut at any specific future meeting is historically quite low. Without current signs of an impending crash scheduled specifically for January 2026, the probability remains close to the historical base rate of such large, single-meeting cuts, which is below 5%.
